nodes.name: Secretary of HHS
nodes.description: The authority prescribing regulations to carry out the purposes of section 290dd-2
nodes.label: FederalAuthority

nodes.name: Patient
nodes.description: Individual receiving SUD treatment who retains rights under 45 CFR 164.522
nodes.label: Patient

nodes.name: Part 2 Program
nodes.description: A federally assisted program or entity providing SUD diagnosis, treatment, or referral
nodes.label: Part2Program

nodes.name: Covered Entity
nodes.description: A HIPAA-aligned entity that may receive records for treatment, payment, or operations
nodes.label: CoveredEntity

nodes.name: Business Associate
nodes.description: An entity working with a covered entity that may receive records
nodes.label: BusinessAssociate

nodes.name: Qualified Service Organization
nodes.description: A person providing services to a Part 2 program under a written agreement
nodes.label: QualifiedServiceOrganization

nodes.name: Intermediary
nodes.description: Entity that receives records under a general designation to disclose to member participants
nodes.label: Intermediary

nodes.name: Lawful Holder
nodes.description: An entity that legally receives protected information downstream
nodes.label: LawfulHolder

nodes.name: Third-Party Payer
nodes.description: An entity funding health plans that may be designated as a recipient
nodes.label: ThirdPartyPayer

nodes.name: Investigative Agency
nodes.description: Agency seeking records via specialized court order for legal or criminal investigations
nodes.label: InvestigativeAgency

nodes.name: Medical Personnel
nodes.description: Personnel permitted to receive disclosures during a medical emergency
nodes.label: MedicalPersonnel

nodes.name: Treating Provider
nodes.description: Medical professional providing care
nodes.label: TreatingProvider

nodes.name: Central Registry
nodes.description: Registry that tracks specific patient treatment data
nodes.label: CentralRegistry

nodes.name: Substance Use Disorder Patient Record
nodes.description: Records maintained in connection with the performance of any part 2 program
nodes.label: Record

nodes.name: Patient Identifying Information
nodes.description: Data that identifies someone as an SUD patient which must be protected from disclosure
nodes.label: PatientIdentifyingInformation

nodes.name: SUD Counseling Notes
nodes.description: Information excluded from standard records which require separate consent mechanics
nodes.label: SudCounselingNotes

nodes.name: Breached Record
nodes.description: A record subject to notification requirements under Section 2.16
nodes.label: UnsecuredRecord

nodes.name: Written Consent
nodes.description: Authorizes disclosure and must include an expiration date.
nodes.label: WrittenConsent

nodes.name: Court Order
nodes.description: A specialized node that forces disclosure and overrides confidentiality restrictions.
nodes.label: CourtOrder

nodes.name: Security Policy
nodes.description: A policy that a Part 2 Program must implement and protect records with.
nodes.label: SecurityPolicy

nodes.name: Notice of Privacy Practices
nodes.description: A required operational privacy notice control.
nodes.label: NoticeOfPrivacyPractices

nodes.name: Medical Emergency Exception
nodes.description: Permits disclosure to medical personnel.
nodes.label: MedicalEmergencyException

nodes.name: Scientific Research Exception
nodes.description: An exception node that applies to permit lawful disclosure.
nodes.label: ScientificResearchException

nodes.name: Confidentiality Restriction
nodes.description: Strict restrictions upon the use and disclosure of records that operate on a default deny logic.
nodes.label: ConfidentialityRestriction

nodes.name: Complaint of Noncompliance
nodes.description: Process allowing patients to file a complaint to the Secretary for violations of the regulation.
nodes.label: Complaint

nodes.name: Treatment Payment and Operations
nodes.description: A specific description of purpose when a patient provides consent for these broader operational uses.
nodes.label: Purpose
